text: The role of multicomponent reactions (MCRs) in synthesis has been subject to considerable debate over recent years. With respect to their significance, possible reaction mechanisms and the current controversial issues in the field of MCRs, this manuscript discusses the five basic multicomponent reactions Biginelli, Hantzsch, Mannich, Passerini and Ugi reactions in detail with the aim of reaching an integral concept from the kinetic and intermedate points of view based on existing experimental evidence. Special emphasis is given on controversial issues in the field of MCRs and possible futures in this area.
